summary: Closed, unmerged HSD/baselib matching slice focused mainly on src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3AA7.c, with one reported new match for fn_803AC3F8, partial-score improvements for fn_803ADF90, fn_803AE7F8, and fn_803B0E9C, a small regression in fn_803B21E8, header prototype/type updates, and mostly formatting-only edits in psdisp.c and a few game/stage files. The author later closed it as superseded by PR #2512.

key_files.role: Primary decomp/matching target; added local structs and large C bodies for CARD/HSD routines including fn_803ADF90, fn_803AE7F8, and fn_803B0E9C; cleaned fn_803AC3F8 into a matched loop form.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3AA7.c +845/-83, 12 hunks
raw_diff: added HsdCmdEntry, Hsd803B2550Ctx, CardStateExt, CMD_QUEUE, and hsd_3B2B include
raw_diff: fn_803AC3F8 changed idx to file_idx and replaced zero-fill unroll block with for loop

key_files.role: Header/prototype updates for hsd_3AA7 routines and partial struct layout recovery.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3AA7.h +15/-10, 3 hunks
raw_diff: hsd_803AAA48 changed from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to s32 hsd_803AAA48(void)
raw_diff: fn_803AD16C changed to s32 fn_803AD16C(CardState* state)
raw_diff: fn_803ADF90/fn_803AE7F8/fn_803AF3F0/fn_803B0120/fn_803B21E8 prototypes changed to use struct hsd_803AC3E0_arg0_t* and named parameters

key_files.role: Formatting/include-order cleanup in psDispParticles and related long GX/math expressions; no bot-reported match item for this file in loaded evidence.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/sysdolphin/baselib/psdisp.c +275/-102, 27 hunks
raw_diff: baselib/forward.h moved after cobj/fog includes with blank-line grouping
raw_diff: long calls and expressions in psDispParticles split across lines

key_files.role: Formatting-only compound-literal spacing for GXColor initializers.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/gm/gm_18A5.c +3/-3, 1 hunk
raw_diff: GXColor casts changed from (GXColor){...} to (GXColor) {...}
changed_files: src/melee/gm/gmclassic.c +3/-3, 1 hunk
raw_diff: casts changed from u8(*)(...) and f32(*)(...) to u8 (*)(...) and f32 (*)(...)
changed_files: src/melee/gr/gricemt.c +1/-1, 1 hunk
raw_diff: HSD_GObjEvent callback cast spacing changed to s32 (*)(HSD_GObj*, s32*)

what_changed: Added hsd_3B2B.h include in hsd_3AA7.c and introduced local temporary structures HsdCmdEntry, Hsd803B2550Ctx, and CardStateExt plus CMD_QUEUE macro for offsets/state fields.
Changed CardQueueEntry.x4 from s32 to void* while preserving s32 use at fn_803AA790 by casting entry->x4 back to s32.

Reworked fn_803AC3F8: renamed idx parameter to file_idx, removed remaining/unroll locals, used typed arg0 assignment, and replaced a generated-looking zero-fill do/while/unroll shape with a short for loop.
Implemented or partially implemented large hsd_3AA7.c routines previously represented by placeholder comments, including fn_803ADF90, fn_803AE7F8, and fn_803B0E9C in the loaded diff excerpt.
Updated hsd_3AA7.h prototypes from unknown or all-s32 signatures toward typed CardState/struct hsd_803AC3E0_arg0_t pointer signatures and named parameters.
Reformatted psdisp.c include grouping and long expressions/calls in psDispParticles.
Applied formatting-only cast/compound-literal spacing changes in gm_18A5.c, gmclassic.c, and gricemt.c.
Bot report recorded one new exact match, three unmatched-item improvements, and one unmatched-item regression in hsd_3AA7.

smart_moves.text: The fn_803AC3F8 cleanup replaced a bulky generated-looking zero-fill sequence with a compact for loop while the bot reported the function reached 100.00%. This is good evidence that testing natural loop forms can improve both readability and match quality.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_803AC3F8 removed remaining/unroll zero-fill block and added for (; i < 3; i++) zero-fill loop
text_corpus: bot report lists fn_803AC3F8 as 82.55% -> 100.00%

smart_moves.text: Header prototypes were updated alongside body/type recovery instead of leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for recovered routines.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_803AAA48 and fn_803AD16C prototype updates in hsd_3AA7.h
raw_diff: typed hsd_803AC3E0_arg0_t* prototypes for several hsd_3AA7 routines

smart_moves.text: The PR kept partially matched functions annotated by score/todo comments rather than claiming exact matches for fn_803AC168, fn_803AC6B8, and fn_803ADE4C.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: @todo Currently 91.67% match for fn_803AC168
raw_diff: @todo Currently 76.6% match for fn_803AC6B8
raw_diff: @todo Currently 93.46% match for fn_803ADE4C

decomp_lessons.lesson: For short repeated byte writes, try a natural counted loop before preserving generated unroll scaffolding; in this slice, removing the unrolled zero-fill tail in fn_803AC3F8 coincided with a bot-reported 100% match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_803AC3F8 zero-fill unroll removed

decomp_lessons.lesson: When recovering body signatures in a matching PR, update the owning header prototypes instead of leaving unknown signatures, but keep evidence narrow to the recovered functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_803AAA48 UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS replaced with s32 hsd_803AAA48(void)
raw_diff: fn_803AD16C and fn_803B21E8 prototype updates

naming_conventions.convention: Use short local role names for recovered storage and flow when the role is locally visible, such as file_idx, file_desc, blocks_before, file_blocks, block_map, callback_seq, seq_num, data_block, primary, secondary, free_blocks, and current_seq.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_803AC3F8 parameter renamed idx to file_idx and local file_desc retained
raw_diff: fn_803ADF90 and fn_803AE7F8 introduced block/file/sequence role locals

naming_conventions.convention: For unrecovered global/function names, the PR retained address-style names such as fn_803ADF90, fn_803AE7F8, fn_803B0E9C, hsd_804D7998, and hsd_804D799C rather than inventing semantic public names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added bodies/prototypes still use fn_803ADF90/fn_803AE7F8/fn_803B0E9C and hsd_804D79xx globals

naming_conventions.convention: Formatting changes used project-style spacing for function-pointer casts and compound-literal casts: e.g. s32 (*)(...), u8 (*)(...), and (GXColor) {...}.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gmclassic.c and gricemt.c function-pointer cast spacing
raw_diff: gm_18A5.c GXColor compound-literal spacing

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Natural-loop replacement for generated-looking unrolled writes in fn_803AC3F8.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed remaining/unroll do-while zeroing 0x20 chunks
raw_diff: added for (; i < 3; i++) zero-fill loop
text_corpus: fn_803AC3F8 reached 100.00%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use temporary internal structs with known offsets/arrays to express CARD state access instead of pervasive raw pointer arithmetic.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added CardStateExt with fields x0, x4, x8, file_info, x20, x24, x28[9], x4C[9], x70[9], x170[64], x270[64], x370, x3B0, x460
raw_diff: fn_803ADF90 and fn_803AE7F8 cast arg0 to CardStateExt* and use state-> fields

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: For async CARD paths, command buffers were modeled as local s32 cmd[9] arrays initialized to zero and then filled by slot before calling fn_803AC168.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_803ADF90 uses s32 cmd[9] = { 0 } with cmd[0], cmd[1], cmd[4], cmd[6], cmd[7], cmd[8]
raw_diff: fn_803AE7F8 and fn_803B0E9C contain similar cmd[9] patterns

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: The slice documents unresolved codegen issues directly in @todo comments where functions are not exact matches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_803AC168 todo mentions volatile load scheduling in critical section
raw_diff: fn_803AC6B8 todo mentions mwcc combined rlwimi
raw_diff: fn_803ADE4C todo mentions stwx vs stw+disp and register swap

accepted_candidate_records.title: Natural short loops can beat generated unroll scaffolding
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2478, fn_803AC3F8 removed a generated-looking remaining/unroll zero-fill block and used a compact counted for loop for the remaining 4-byte records; the bot report showed the function became a 100.00% match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_803AC3F8 zero-fill unroll replaced by for loop
text_corpus: bot report fn_803AC3F8 82.55% -> 100.00%
